---
title: "Boston Housing — Exploratory Data Analysis"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
execute:
  echo: true
  warning: false
  message: false
---

## 1) Set Up Environment (Python + Quarto)

If you haven’t installed packages yet:

```bash
pip install -U pandas numpy matplotlib seaborn scipy scikit-learn pyyaml
```

Verify Quarto:

```bash
quarto --version
```

## 2) Load + Validate Data

```{python}
from __future__ import annotations

import json
import os
from pathlib import Path

import numpy as np
import pandas as pd

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns

np.random.seed(0)

DATA_PATH = Path('data/boston.csv')
OUTPUT_DIR = Path('outputs/boston_eda_qmd')
OUTPUT_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

EXPECTED_COLUMNS = [
    'CRIM','ZN','INDUS','CHAS','NOX','RM','AGE','DIS','RAD',
    'TAX','PTRATIO','B','LSTAT','MEDV'
]

print('cwd:', Path.cwd())
print('data exists:', DATA_PATH.exists(), DATA_PATH.resolve())

df = pd.read_csv(DATA_PATH)
print('shape:', df.shape)
print('missing total:', int(df.isna().sum().sum()))
print('duplicates:', int(df.duplicated().sum()))

missing_expected = [c for c in EXPECTED_COLUMNS if c not in df.columns]
extra_cols = [c for c in df.columns if c not in EXPECTED_COLUMNS]
print('missing expected columns:', missing_expected)
print('extra columns:', extra_cols)

df.head()
```

## 3) Summary Tables

```{python}
import numpy as np

numeric_cols = df.select_dtypes(include=[np.number]).columns.tolist()
desc = df[numeric_cols].describe().T

desc
```

```{python}
if 'CHAS' in df.columns:
    df['CHAS'].value_counts(dropna=False)
```

```{python}
if 'CHAS' in df.columns and 'MEDV' in df.columns:
    df.groupby('CHAS', dropna=False)['MEDV'].agg(['count','mean','median','std','min','max'])
```

## 4) Correlations

```{python}
pearson = df.corr(numeric_only=True)['MEDV'].sort_values(ascending=False)
spearman = df.corr(numeric_only=True, method='spearman')['MEDV'].sort_values(ascending=False)

pd.DataFrame({'pearson': pearson, 'spearman': spearman}).round(3)
```

## 5) Visualizations (saved to disk too)

```{python}
sns.set_theme(style='whitegrid')

def savefig(name: str) -> Path:
    path = OUTPUT_DIR / name
    plt.savefig(path, dpi=200, bbox_inches='tight')
    print('saved:', path)
    return path

plt.figure(figsize=(10, 8))
sns.heatmap(df.corr(numeric_only=True), cmap='vlag', center=0, square=True)
plt.title('Correlation heatmap')
plt.tight_layout()
savefig('corr_heatmap.png')
plt.show()
```

```{python}
plt.figure(figsize=(8, 5))
sns.histplot(df['MEDV'], bins=30, kde=True)
plt.title('MEDV distribution')
plt.tight_layout()
savefig('medv_hist.png')
plt.show()
```

```{python}
for x in ['RM', 'LSTAT', 'PTRATIO', 'NOX']:
    plt.figure(figsize=(6.5, 5))
    sns.scatterplot(data=df, x=x, y='MEDV', alpha=0.7, s=18)
    sns.regplot(data=df, x=x, y='MEDV', scatter=False, color='black', line_kws={'lw': 1})
    plt.title(f'MEDV vs {x}')
    plt.tight_layout()
    savefig(f'scatter_{x.lower()}_medv.png')
    plt.show()
```

## 6) Render

Convert from notebook (optional):

```bash
quarto convert sandbox/boston_eda.ipynb --output sandbox/boston_eda_from_ipynb.qmd
```

Render this `.qmd`:

```bash
quarto render sandbox/boston_eda.qmd
```
